text: Chapter 20 teaches voice-leading transformations and substitution techniques specific to rock harmony. The master's characteristic moves include lowering the 3rd and b7th of dominant chords by one fret to generate IV9 chords — a transformation that generalizes across any dominant and can be applied progressively through cycle-based dominant series — and using only the 3rd and b7 shell voicing as a melodic foundation. Two additional techniques define the chapter: the resolving diminished chord (constructed from any three notes of a diminished 7th chord one fret above the V7's root, resolving to I or m(maj7)) and the I-IV-I sandwich pattern, where the IV chord sits between two I chords, extended via cycle motion by inserting the IV-of-IV before the IV. The chapter also establishes the stylistic principle that major chords in rock can substitute dominant 7th chords and their extensions.
